decisions.summary: Supervisor runs under Node 22, not Bun.
decisions.rationale: @homebridge/node-pty-prebuilt-multiarch is a native Node addon. Under Bun's addon compat layer the PTY spawns but exits code 0 immediately (documented in CLAUDE.md and the dashboard-node22-only rule). The supervisor *owns* the PTY master fd so it cannot be Bun. Bridge stays Bun because it doesn't need a PTY.

Matches the existing channels bridge convention (`sockets/agent-<id>.sock`) so dashboard/CLI/log code can use one socket directory and identical permissioning. The `pty-` prefix keeps the two sockets distinguishable.

decisions.summary: Wire format is HTTP-on-unix with a per-agent pty-token header.
decisions.rationale: Symmetric with panopticon-bridge so deliverAgentMessage's HTTP-over-unix POST helper (postUnixSocketJson) is reused. New helper src/lib/pty-token.ts mirrors bridge-token.ts; tokens never live in env or argv, only in 0600 files under the agent state dir.

decisions.summary: Supervisor echoes socket-delivered messages to its stdout (and therefore to the tmux pane) by default.
decisions.rationale: Transcript completeness > secrecy. Operators looking at the tmux pane should see what Cloister sent, just as if they had typed it. A per-message `{ echo: false }` field is reserved for a future silent-delivery case but is not used by any caller in this issue.

decisions.summary: deliverAgentMessage becomes a three-tier router: supervisor → channels → tmux.
decisions.rationale: Already-running agents (channelsEnabled=true) keep working because supervisor socket is absent → fallback to channels socket → tmux. New agents get pty socket first. No forced respawn.

decisions.summary: Channels MCP becomes off-by-default for new spawns; the supervisor replaces it as the preferred transport.
decisions.rationale: Channels MCP was Anthropic-only (provider.name === 'anthropic' gate), pays an MCP-tool-schema context tax, and requires dismissDevChannelsDialog. Supervisor delivers reliably across every Claude Code provider with zero MCP context cost. Channels code path stays for delivery fallback only; new spawns do not wire --dangerously-load-development-channels.

decisions.summary: Plain forks: re-apply the 'skip channels MCP wiring' policy (the reverted 1d621196c reasoning is correct) and add supervisor delivery instead.
decisions.rationale: Original plain-fork concerns (MCP tool-schema pushes resume across auto-compact threshold; dismissDevChannelsDialog can confirm an auto-compact prompt by accident) still apply. With the supervisor providing a reliable side-channel, plain forks finally have a delivery path that doesn't depend on channels MCP.

decisions.summary: Supervisor is built to dist/pty-supervisor.js via tsdown; launcher invokes `node <projectRoot>/dist/pty-supervisor.js claude ...`.
decisions.rationale: Node 22 cannot run TS sources directly (no top-level Bun, ESM circular-deps land on the dashboard server but the supervisor must also work cleanly), so we ship a built artifact. Add @homebridge/node-pty-prebuilt-multiarch to the root package.json with node-pty marked `external` in the new tsdown entry so the build doesn't bundle the native addon.

decisions.summary: Docker workspaces stay on the existing eligibility gate — supervisor is not enabled for Docker yet.
decisions.rationale: Host↔container shared-socket plumbing is unsolved (same reason channels gates Docker off today). Defer to a follow-up issue; document in supervisor docs and re-use the existing PAN_DOCKER / PANOPTICON_DOCKER_WORKSPACE env-var gate.

decisions.summary: Permission-decision relay (bidirectional tool-call mediation) is OUT OF SCOPE; existing channels MCP path keeps owning it.
decisions.rationale: The issue body lists bidirectional tool-call mediation as out-of-scope. Supervisor is one-way (orchestrator→agent). Permission decisions continue to flow through the existing channels socket for agents that still wire channels MCP; new spawns without channels MCP will not request permissions via that path (they rely on Claude's built-in permission flags).

decisions.summary: Pi runtime is untouched — Pi continues to use its rpc.in FIFO (src/lib/runtimes/pi-fifo.ts).
decisions.rationale: Pi already solved its own delivery via FIFO. Adding the supervisor on top would duplicate work and obscure failure modes. Eligibility check filters on harness === 'claude-code' so Pi never enters the supervisor path.

decisions.summary: Existing tmux paste-buffer scroll-mode lockout is the canonical UAT signal: when the dashboard terminal panel is scrolled up, supervisor-delivered messages must still reach Claude.
decisions.rationale: This is the user-visible problem motivating the issue. UAT writes a Playwright test that opens the terminal panel, scrolls up to put tmux into copy-mode, sends a Cloister message, and asserts the agent's transcript advanced. Without this assertion the supervisor's reason-for-existing is unverified.

hazards.summary: Supervisor crash kills Claude because the supervisor owns the PTY master fd.
hazards.mitigation: Document explicitly in supervisor.ts header. Resume via the dashboard regenerates launcher + supervisor — same as a fresh spawn. The Deacon's recoverOrphanedAgents path treats this case the same as any other dead tmux session.

hazards.summary: node-pty must be present at the root package.json AND must build cleanly for the supervisor entry.
hazards.mitigation: Add @homebridge/node-pty-prebuilt-multiarch to root deps. Add a tsdown entry that emits dist/pty-supervisor.js with node-pty marked `external`. Verify `npm run build` produces the file before merging.

hazards.summary: Supervisor stdout backpressure: if claude's output outpaces tmux's read rate, supervisor's stdout pipe could fill.
hazards.mitigation: Use Node streams correctly — listen to `process.stdout.write` return value, pause node-pty's data flow until `drain` fires. Add a log line if backpressure trips so we can observe it in the field. Defer formal load-testing to a follow-up unless UAT reveals dropped bytes.

hazards.summary: Docker workspaces do not yet share the supervisor socket between host and container.
hazards.mitigation: Eligibility gate (PAN_DOCKER / PANOPTICON_DOCKER_WORKSPACE) skips the supervisor — Docker conversations stay on tmux as today. Document in supervisor.ts header and CLAUDE.md.

hazards.summary: Existing agents with channelsEnabled=true must keep working — the three-tier router must fall through cleanly when the supervisor socket is absent.
hazards.mitigation: deliver-agent-message.test.ts adds explicit cases: (a) only channels socket exists → routes to channels, (b) neither exists → routes to tmux, (c) both exist → prefers supervisor. Test resume of a pre-supervisor agent doesn't regress.

hazards.summary: Re-applying the plain-fork channels-skip behavior (the recent revert) must happen ATOMICALLY with supervisor enablement.
hazards.mitigation: Land the plain-fork bead AFTER the supervisor-wiring bead in the same release. Add an integration test that spawns a plain fork and asserts (i) no panopticon-bridge MCP in the launcher script, (ii) supervisor socket present and POSTable.

hazards.summary: Supervisor must inherit and forward TTY-rendering env vars (TERM, COLORTERM, LANG, NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS, PATH) so Claude renders correctly under the wrapped PTY.
hazards.mitigation: Supervisor passes through process.env unchanged to the spawned claude. Launcher continues to export TERM/COLORTERM/LANG/LC_ALL before invoking node. Unit test asserts env propagation.

hazards.summary: Resize events from the dashboard terminal panel must reach Claude through the supervisor.
hazards.mitigation: Supervisor listens to its own stdout `resize` events (via process.stdout.on('resize')) and calls pty.resize(cols, rows). Tmux already propagates SIGWINCH into the pane's foreground process; the supervisor is that process, so this path is the standard one. Unit test simulates a resize and asserts pty.resize was called.

hazards.summary: Token file races: the launcher writes pty-token before tmux spawn, but deliverAgentMessage may run before the supervisor has bound the socket.
hazards.mitigation: deliverAgentMessage already handles socket-missing as a fallback — the three-tier router falls through to channels (legacy) or tmux. Add a small grace-period (existing 30s ready-check in spawnAgent already covers this for the initial prompt). No race-prone polling needed.

hazards.summary: Dev-mode (running from source) vs prod-mode (running from dist/) supervisor path resolution.
hazards.mitigation: Launcher generator resolves an absolute supervisor path passed in by the caller — caller resolves dist/pty-supervisor.js relative to the project root. If dist is missing, spawnAgent / spawnConversationSession surface a clear error: 'pty-supervisor build artifact missing — run `npm run build`'. Don't silently fall back to tmux for new spawns.

hazards.summary: Skipping dismissDevChannelsDialog when channels MCP is not wired is correct but easy to get wrong.
hazards.mitigation: Gate the dialog dismissal on the same boolean (channelsBridgeMcpConfig != null OR state.channelsEnabled). Add an assertion test that for a supervisor-only spawn, dismissDevChannelsDialog is never called.

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1405: Universal PTY supervisor for message delivery (replace tmux paste-buffer + obsolete channels MCP gate)
Completed discovery in --auto mode. 12 decisions and 11 hazards recorded; vBRIEF drafted with 12 beads.
